title: Quadlet: consider publishing a standalone, implementation-independent specification (compose-spec model)
body:
### Feature request description

Right now the normative reference for Quadlet is podman-systemd.unit(5), documentation for the Podman CLI/generator itself. That's the right source of truth for Podman users. But for anyone trying to build against Quadlet from outside the Podman project — a third-party parser, a packaging format, or another specification that wants to reference Quadlet as a dependency — there isn't currently a standalone, versioned document to point to. You end up citing a man page tied to a specific Podman release rather than a spec artifact with its own identity and release cadence.

Would the maintainers be open to a standalone Quadlet specification, decoupled from the Podman codebase and versioned independently — similar to how the Compose Specification was split out from Docker Compose into its own repo/org in 2020? Podman's generator (pkg/systemd/quadlet/) would remain the reference implementation; the point would be to give the format a citable surface separate from any one implementation's version number.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

We're contributing to the Linux Foundation's Margo project (an open standard for industrial edge workload orchestration). Margo already supports Helm and Compose as deployment profile types, and we've proposed Quadlet as a third profile — that proposal is under review with the Margo Technical Working Group and isn't ratified yet, so nothing here assumes it will be accepted. If it is, Margo's spec would need something concrete to cite for "what a conformant Quadlet component looks like" — ideally a document with its own version number and change history, not "whatever podman-systemd.unit(5) says as of Podman 5.x."

We're not the only ones parsing/generating Quadlet syntax outside podman itself — podlet and the Podman Desktop Quadlet extension already do — so external interest in the format doesn't seem unique to our use case.

[quadlet-spec-v1.0.0.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/30196056/quadlet-spec-v1.0.0.md)



### Suggest potential solution

A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.Open to whatever shape the maintainers think fits the project best, but two options that come to mind:

Standalone repo, e.g. containers/quadlet-spec, holding a versioned spec.md (unit types, grammar, generated-service defaults, conformance levels) with its own release tags — man pages stay as the day-to-day user reference and would defer to the spec for the underlying grammar.
In-tree but explicitly decoupled: keep the document inside containers/podman but give it its own version number, changelog, and a clear "reference implementation wins on conflict" statement, so external projects can pin to a spec version without pinning to a Podman release.




### Have you considered any alternatives?

Citing podman-systemd.unit(5) directly, pinned to a specific Podman version — works, but ties every external adopter's compatibility statement to Podman's release cadence rather than to the format itself.
Each external project reverse-engineering and vendoring its own description of Quadlet syntax from the generator source — duplicative and prone to drifting out of sync.

### Additional context

Either way, defined conformance levels (minimal parsing vs. full systemd-integration behavior vs. bit-identical output) would help third-party implementers know what they're signing up for.

comments.body:
There are a lot of new keys added all the time as it has for from all podman options yet. And of course we can add new podman cli options as well and then need new quadlet options. Maintaining a separate repo where someone need to bump this thing each time for each option seems very undesirable from a maintainer POV. How would that help any podman maintainer/contributor.

I at least would not be in favour of taking on the maintenance load. If someone else wants to create a spec.

cc @ygalblum
